name: Kimi k1.5
archDisplay: Transformer, RL-trained long/short-CoT reasoning (parameter count undisclosed)
note: Moonshot's first RL-trained multimodal reasoning model, claiming OpenAI o1-level math, coding, and vision performance.

results.notes: MMLU (EM) = 87.4, Kimi k1.5 short-CoT model, from Table 3 of the arXiv paper (v4 HTML), which compares the short-CoT model against Qwen2.5-72B-Inst., LLaMA-3.1-405B-Inst., DeepSeek-V3, Claude-3.5-Sonnet-1022 and GPT-4o-0513.
IF-Eval (Prompt Strict) = 87.2, Kimi k1.5 short-CoT model, Table 3.
C-Eval (EM) = 88.3, Kimi k1.5 short-CoT model, Table 3.

MMMU-Val (Pass@1) = 70.0, Kimi k1.5 long-CoT model, from Table 2 (the same table the existing MathVista/AIME/MATH-500 long-CoT entries come from). The short-CoT model scores 68.0 on the same benchmark per Table 3; long-CoT figure chosen for consistency with the model's other existing long-CoT entries.
MathVision-Full (Pass@1) = 38.6, Kimi k1.5 long-CoT model, Table 2 (short-CoT model scores 31.0 per Table 3).

verificationNotes: Confirmed genuine via arXiv:2501.12599 ('Kimi k1.5: Scaling Reinforcement Learning with LLMs', submitted 2025-01-20, matches releaseDate) and MoonshotAI/Kimi-k1.5 GitHub repo. All four benchmark rows (AIME 77.5, MATH-500 96.2, LiveCodeBench 47.3, MathVista 74.9) independently re-fetched from the exact sourceUrl and confirmed to literally match. apiAvailable:false confirmed correct — k1.5 was a research release; Moonshot's current public API model list (platform.kimi.ai) does not list a kimi-k1.5 model ID. No changes needed.
